contents:
(** Here we characterize the condition for which a sigma type over an injective type is itself injective, and we also provide some examples.

Many proofs guided by original Agda formalization in the Type Topology Library which can be found at: https://martinescardo.github.io/TypeTopology/InjectiveTypes.Sigma and InjectiveTypes.MathematicalStructuresMoreGeneral. *)
Context {X : Type} (A : X -> Type) (Xaf : IsAlgebraicFlabbyType X).
(** The condition for a sigma type over an algebraically flabby type to be algebraically flabby can be described as the existence of a section to the following map for every [P] and [f]. *)

Definition alg_flab_map (P : HProp) (f : P -> X)
    : (A (center_af f)) -> (forall h, A (f h))
    := fun a h => contr_af f h # a.
(** Here is the condition.  (It would in fact be enough to assume that [s] is section up to homotopies in its domain.) *)

Definition alg_flab_sigma_condition
    := forall P f, {s : _ & alg_flab_map P f o s == idmap}.
(** A sigma type over an algebraically flabby type is also algebraically flabby, and thus algebraically injective, when the above condition holds. *)

Definition alg_flab_sigma (cond : alg_flab_sigma_condition)
    : IsAlgebraicFlabbyType {x : X & A x}.

goals.conclusion: IsAlgebraicFlabbyType {x : X & A x}
IsAlgebraicFlabbyType {x : X & A x}
{x : X & A x}

forall p : P,
(fun (P0 : HProp) (f0 : P0 -> {x : X & A x}) => ?Goal@{P:=P0; f:=f0}) P f =
f p

contents: all: pose (C := cond P (pr1 o f)).

goals.conclusion: {x : X & A x}

hypotheses.type:
{s : (forall h : P, A ((pr1 o f) h)) -> A (center_af (pr1 o f)) &
alg_flab_map P (pr1 o f) o s == idmap}

goals.conclusion:
forall p : P,
(fun (P0 : HProp) (f0 : P0 -> {x : X & A x}) =>
 let C0 := cond P0 (pr1 o f0) in ?Goal@{P:=P0; f:=f0}) P f =
f p

hypotheses.type:
{s : (forall h : P, A ((pr1 o f) h)) -> A (center_af (pr1 o f)) &
alg_flab_map P (pr1 o f) o s == idmap}

goals.conclusion: {x : X & A x}

hypotheses.type:
{s : (forall h : P, A ((pr1 o f) h)) -> A (center_af (pr1 o f)) &
alg_flab_map P (pr1 o f) o s == idmap}

contents: (* We need to give a point in [{x : X * A x}].  Since [X] is algebraically flabby, we get a point in [X]: *)

goals.conclusion: A (center_af (fun x : P => (f x).1))

hypotheses.type:
{s : (forall h : P, A ((pr1 o f) h)) -> A (center_af (pr1 o f)) &
alg_flab_map P (pr1 o f) o s == idmap}

contents: (* For the point in [A (above)], we use the section component of [cond P (pr1 o f)]: *)

goals.conclusion:
forall p : P,
(fun (P0 : HProp) (f0 : P0 -> {x : X & A x}) =>
 let C0 := cond P0 (pr1 o f0) in
 (center_af (fun x : P0 => (f0 x).1); C0.1 (pr2 o f0))) P f =
f p

hypotheses.type:
{s : (forall h : P, A ((pr1 o f) h)) -> A (center_af (pr1 o f)) &
alg_flab_map P (pr1 o f) o s == idmap}

{s : (forall h0 : P, A ((pr1 o f) h0)) -> A (center_af (pr1 o f)) &
alg_flab_map P (pr1 o f) o s == idmap}

contents: change (cond P (fun x : P => (f x).1)) with C.

goals.conclusion: (center_af (fun x : P => (f x).1); C.1 (fun x : P => (f x).2)) = f h

hypotheses.type:
{s : (forall h0 : P, A ((pr1 o f) h0)) -> A (center_af (pr1 o f)) &
alg_flab_map P (pr1 o f) o s == idmap}

contents: (* We need to show that the point we gave is equal to [f h]. *)

goals.conclusion: center_af (fun x : P => (f x).1) = (f h).1

hypotheses.type:
{s : (forall h0 : P, A ((pr1 o f) h0)) -> A (center_af (pr1 o f)) &
alg_flab_map P (pr1 o f) o s == idmap}

goals.conclusion: transport (fun x : X => A x) ?Goal (C.1 (fun x : P => (f x).2)) = (f h).2

hypotheses.type:
{s : (forall h0 : P, A ((pr1 o f) h0)) -> A (center_af (pr1 o f)) &
alg_flab_map P (pr1 o f) o s == idmap}

goals.conclusion: center_af (fun x : P => (f x).1) = (f h).1

hypotheses.type:
{s : (forall h0 : P, A ((pr1 o f) h0)) -> A (center_af (pr1 o f)) &
alg_flab_map P (pr1 o f) o s == idmap}

goals.conclusion:
transport (fun x : X => A x) (contr_af (fun x : P => (f x).1) h)
  (C.1 (fun x : P => (f x).2)) =
(f h).2

hypotheses.type:
{s : (forall h0 : P, A ((pr1 o f) h0)) -> A (center_af (pr1 o f)) &
alg_flab_map P (pr1 o f) o s == idmap}

contents: (** Taking our algebraically flabby type [X] to be [Type], we introduce our primary examples (the types of pointed types, monoids, etc.), and give a few simplifying lemmas. *)

(** [T] is a version of transport in the type family [A].  It could be defined to be transport combined with univalence, but we allow the user to choose different "implementations" of transport that might be more convenient. *)

Context (A : Type -> Type) (T : forall {X Y}, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y)
    (Trefl : forall X, (T (equiv_idmap X) == idmap)).

(* This is needed, despite the braces above. *)

  (** When studying the sigma type [{X : Type & A X}], the map [alg_flab_map] can be exchanged for either of these simpler maps.  This first one will turn out to be homotopic to [alg_flab_map] when [Type] is given its "forall" flabbiness structure. *)

goals.conclusion: A (forall h : P, f h) -> forall h : P, A (f h)

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y

goals.conclusion: A (forall h : P, f h) -> forall h : P, A (f h)

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y

goals.conclusion: A (f h)

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y

goals.conclusion: (forall h0 : P, f h0) <~> f h

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y

contents: (** And this one is homotopic to [alg_flab_map] when [Type] is given its "sigma" flabbiness structure. *)

Definition alg_flab_map_sigma
    (P : HProp) (f : P -> Type)
    : A {h : P & f h} -> (forall h, A (f h)).

goals.conclusion: A {h : P & f h} -> forall h : P, A (f h)

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y

goals.conclusion: A {h : P & f h} -> forall h : P, A (f h)

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y

goals.conclusion: A (f h)

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y

goals.conclusion: {h0 : P & f h0} <~> f h

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y

contents: (** The following conditions can be thought of as closure conditions under pi or sigma types for the type family [A]. *)

Definition alg_flab_sigma_condition_forall `{Funext}
    := forall P f, {s : _ & alg_flab_map_forall P f o s == idmap}.

Definition alg_flab_sigma_condition_sigma
    := forall P f, {s : _ & alg_flab_map_sigma P f o s == idmap}.

goals.conclusion: alg_flab_map_forall P f == alg_flab_map A alg_flab_Type_forall P f

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y

goals.conclusion: alg_flab_map_forall P f == alg_flab_map A alg_flab_Type_forall P f

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y

goals.conclusion: alg_flab_map_forall P f a = alg_flab_map A alg_flab_Type_forall P f a

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y

goals.conclusion: alg_flab_map_forall P f a h = alg_flab_map A alg_flab_Type_forall P f a h

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y

contents: srefine (homotopic_trequiv A (@T) (@univalent_transport H A) Trefl _ _ a).

goals.conclusion: forall X : Type, univalent_transport A 1 == idmap

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y

goals.conclusion: alg_flab_map_sigma P f == alg_flab_map A alg_flab_Type_sigma P f

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y

goals.conclusion: alg_flab_map_sigma P f == alg_flab_map A alg_flab_Type_sigma P f

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y

goals.conclusion: alg_flab_map_sigma P f s = alg_flab_map A alg_flab_Type_sigma P f s

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y

goals.conclusion: alg_flab_map_sigma P f s h = alg_flab_map A alg_flab_Type_sigma P f s h

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y

contents: srefine (homotopic_trequiv A (@T) (@univalent_transport H A) Trefl _ _ s).

goals.conclusion: forall X : Type, univalent_transport A 1 == idmap

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y

contents: (** The original [sigma_condition] is implied by our reformulated conditions [alg_flab_sigma_condition_forall] and [alg_flab_sigma_condition_sigma]. *)

goals.conclusion: alg_flab_sigma_condition A alg_flab_Type_forall

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y

goals.conclusion: alg_flab_sigma_condition A alg_flab_Type_forall

goals.conclusion:
{s : (forall h : P, A (f h)) -> A (center_af f) &
(fun x : forall h : P, A (f h) =>
 alg_flab_map A alg_flab_Type_forall P f (s x)) ==
idmap}

goals.conclusion:
{s0 : (forall h : P, A (f h)) -> A (center_af f) &
(fun x : forall h : P, A (f h) =>
 alg_flab_map A alg_flab_Type_forall P f (s0 x)) ==
idmap}

(forall h : P, A (f h)) -> A (forall h : P, f h)
(fun x : forall h : P, A (f h) => alg_flab_map_forall P f (s x)) == idmap

goals.conclusion:
(fun x : forall h : P, A (f h) =>
 alg_flab_map A alg_flab_Type_forall P f (s x)) ==
idmap

(forall h : P, A (f h)) -> A (forall h : P, f h)
(fun x : forall h : P, A (f h) => alg_flab_map_forall P f (s x)) == idmap

goals.conclusion: alg_flab_map A alg_flab_Type_forall P f (s g) = g

hypotheses.type: forall X Y : Type, X <~> Y -> A X -> A Y
(forall h : P, A (f h)) -> A (forall h : P, f h)
(fun x : forall h : P, A (f h) => alg_flab_map_forall P f (s x)) == idmap

goals.conclusion: alg_flab_map_forall P f (s g) = g

(forall h : P, A (f h)) -> A (forall h : P, f h)
(fun x : forall h : P, A (f h) => alg_flab_map_forall P f (s x)) == idmap

Definition alg_flab_sigma_sigma `{Univalence} (conds : alg_flab_sigma_condition_sigma)
    : IsAlgebraicFlabbyType {X : Type & A X}.

goals.conclusion: IsAlgebraicFlabbyType {X : Type & A X}

contents: (** The type of pointed types is algebraically flabby. *)

goals.conclusion: IsAlgebraicFlabbyType {X : Type & X}
IsAlgebraicFlabbyType {X : Type & X}
forall X : Type, 1%equiv == idmap
alg_flab_sigma_condition_forall idmap (@equiv_fun)
forall X : Type, 1%equiv == idmap
alg_flab_sigma_condition_forall idmap (@equiv_fun)

{s : (forall h : P, f h) -> forall h : P, f h &
(fun x : forall h : P, f h =>
 alg_flab_map_forall idmap (@equiv_fun) P f (s x)) ==
idmap}

(fun x : forall h : P, f h => alg_flab_map_forall idmap (@equiv_fun) P f x) ==
idmap

contents:
(** The following results are adapted from section 7 of Injective Types in Univalent Mathematics by Martín Escardó, using the above results. *)

(** For a subuniverse, the flabbiness condition is equivalent to closure under proposition indexed pi types (or sigma types), so using this we can state a simpler theorem for proving flabbiness of subuniverses. *)

Definition alg_flab_subuniverse_forall `{Univalence} (O : Subuniverse)
  (condForall : forall (P : HProp) A, (forall h : P, In O (A h)) -> In O (forall h : P, A h))
  : IsAlgebraicFlabbyType@{u su} (Type_ O).

goals.conclusion: IsAlgebraicFlabbyType (Type_ O)

hypotheses.type:
forall (P : HProp) (A : P -> Type),
(forall h : P, In O (A h)) -> In O (forall h : P, A h)

goals.conclusion: IsAlgebraicFlabbyType (Type_ O)

hypotheses.type:
forall (P : HProp) (A : P -> Type),
(forall h : P, In O (A h)) -> In O (forall h : P, A h)

contents: rapply (alg_flab_sigma_forall _ (fun X Y f H => @inO_equiv_inO' O X Y H f)).

goals.conclusion: forall X : Type, (fun H0 : In O X => inO_equiv_inO' X 1) == idmap

hypotheses.type:
forall (P : HProp) (A : P -> Type),
(forall h : P, In O (A h)) -> In O (forall h : P, A h)

goals.conclusion:
alg_flab_sigma_condition_forall (In O)
  (fun (X Y : Type) (f : X <~> Y) (H0 : In O X) => inO_equiv_inO' X f)

hypotheses.type:
forall (P : HProp) (A : P -> Type),
(forall h : P, In O (A h)) -> In O (forall h : P, A h)

goals.conclusion: forall X : Type, (fun H0 : In O X => inO_equiv_inO' X 1) == idmap

hypotheses.type:
forall (P : HProp) (A : P -> Type),
(forall h : P, In O (A h)) -> In O (forall h : P, A h)

goals.conclusion: inO_equiv_inO' X 1 = A

hypotheses.type:
forall (P : HProp) (A0 : P -> Type),
(forall h : P, In O (A0 h)) -> In O (forall h : P, A0 h)

goals.conclusion:
alg_flab_sigma_condition_forall (In O)
  (fun (X Y : Type) (f : X <~> Y) (H0 : In O X) => inO_equiv_inO' X f)

hypotheses.type:
forall (P : HProp) (A : P -> Type),
(forall h : P, In O (A h)) -> In O (forall h : P, A h)

goals.conclusion:
{s : (forall h : P, In O (A h)) -> In O (forall h : P, A h) &
(fun x : forall h : P, In O (A h) =>
 alg_flab_map_forall (In O)
   (fun (X Y : Type) (f : X <~> Y) (H0 : In O X) => inO_equiv_inO' X f) P A
   (s x)) ==
idmap}

hypotheses.type:
forall (P0 : HProp) (A0 : P0 -> Type),
(forall h : P0, In O (A0 h)) -> In O (forall h : P0, A0 h)

goals.conclusion:
(fun x : forall h : P, In O (A h) =>
 alg_flab_map_forall (In O)
   (fun (X Y : Type) (f : X <~> Y) (H0 : In O X) => inO_equiv_inO' X f) P A
   (condForall P A x)) ==
idmap

hypotheses.type:
forall (P0 : HProp) (A0 : P0 -> Type),
(forall h : P0, In O (A0 h)) -> In O (forall h : P0, A0 h)

goals.conclusion:
alg_flab_map_forall (In O)
  (fun (X Y : Type) (f : X <~> Y) (H0 : In O X) => inO_equiv_inO' X f) P A
  (condForall P A s) =
s

hypotheses.type:
forall (P0 : HProp) (A0 : P0 -> Type),
(forall h : P0, In O (A0 h)) -> In O (forall h : P0, A0 h)
forall h : P, In O (A h)

contents:
Definition alg_flab_subuniverse_sigma `{Univalence} (O : Subuniverse)
  (condSigma : forall (P : HProp) A, (forall h : P, In O (A h)) -> In O {h : P & A h})
  : IsAlgebraicFlabbyType@{u su} (Type_ O).

goals.conclusion: IsAlgebraicFlabbyType (Type_ O)

hypotheses.type:
forall (P : HProp) (A : P -> Type),
(forall h : P, In O (A h)) -> In O {h : P & A h}

goals.conclusion: IsAlgebraicFlabbyType (Type_ O)

hypotheses.type:
forall (P : HProp) (A : P -> Type),
(forall h : P, In O (A h)) -> In O {h : P & A h}

contents: rapply (alg_flab_sigma_sigma _ (fun X Y f H => @inO_equiv_inO' O X Y H f)).

goals.conclusion: forall X : Type, (fun H0 : In O X => inO_equiv_inO' X 1) == idmap

hypotheses.type:
forall (P : HProp) (A : P -> Type),
(forall h : P, In O (A h)) -> In O {h : P & A h}

goals.conclusion:
alg_flab_sigma_condition_sigma (In O)
  (fun (X Y : Type) (f : X <~> Y) (H0 : In O X) => inO_equiv_inO' X f)

hypotheses.type:
forall (P : HProp) (A : P -> Type),
(forall h : P, In O (A h)) -> In O {h : P & A h}

goals.conclusion: forall X : Type, (fun H0 : In O X => inO_equiv_inO' X 1) == idmap

hypotheses.type:
forall (P : HProp) (A : P -> Type),
(forall h : P, In O (A h)) -> In O {h : P & A h}

goals.conclusion: inO_equiv_inO' X 1 = A

hypotheses.type:
forall (P : HProp) (A0 : P -> Type),
(forall h : P, In O (A0 h)) -> In O {h : P & A0 h}

goals.conclusion:
alg_flab_sigma_condition_sigma (In O)
  (fun (X Y : Type) (f : X <~> Y) (H0 : In O X) => inO_equiv_inO' X f)

hypotheses.type:
forall (P : HProp) (A : P -> Type),
(forall h : P, In O (A h)) -> In O {h : P & A h}

goals.conclusion:
{s : (forall h : P, In O (A h)) -> In O {h : P & A h} &
(fun x : forall h : P, In O (A h) =>
 alg_flab_map_sigma (In O)
   (fun (X Y : Type) (f : X <~> Y) (H0 : In O X) => inO_equiv_inO' X f) P A
   (s x)) ==
idmap}

hypotheses.type:
forall (P0 : HProp) (A0 : P0 -> Type),
(forall h : P0, In O (A0 h)) -> In O {h : P0 & A0 h}

goals.conclusion:
(fun x : forall h : P, In O (A h) =>
 alg_flab_map_sigma (In O)
   (fun (X Y : Type) (f : X <~> Y) (H0 : In O X) => inO_equiv_inO' X f) P A
   (condSigma P A x)) ==
idmap

hypotheses.type:
forall (P0 : HProp) (A0 : P0 -> Type),
(forall h : P0, In O (A0 h)) -> In O {h : P0 & A0 h}

goals.conclusion:
alg_flab_map_sigma (In O)
  (fun (X Y : Type) (f : X <~> Y) (H0 : In O X) => inO_equiv_inO' X f) P A
  (condSigma P A s) =
s

hypotheses.type:
forall (P0 : HProp) (A0 : P0 -> Type),
(forall h : P0, In O (A0 h)) -> In O {h : P0 & A0 h}
forall h : P, In O (A h)

contents: (** As an immediate corollary, we get that reflective subuniverses are algebraically flabby. *)

Definition alg_flab_reflective_subuniverse `{Univalence} (O : ReflectiveSubuniverse)
  : IsAlgebraicFlabbyType@{u su} (Type_ O)
  := alg_flab_subuniverse_forall _ _.
